Let $N$ be the number of students who took the test.

So, $28N = 972$.

We have $14N = \sum_{i=1}^9 x_i = \sum_{j=1}^6 y_j$.
Let $x_i = y_j = \frac{14N}{14} = N$.
Then $\sum_{i=1}^9 \sum_{j=1}^6 a_{ij} = \sum_{i=1}^9 \sum_{j=1}^6 a_{ij} = \sum_{i=1}^9 \sum_{j=1}^6 N = 9 \cdot 6 \cdot N = 54N$.
So $54N = 972$, which gives $N = \frac{972}{54} = 18$.

Final Answer: The final answer is $\boxed{18}$
